data.title: MTU Aero Engines Revenue Rises on Strength in Military, Commercial Maintenance Businesses
data.description:
(06:00 GMT) *MTU Aero Engines Backs 2026 View
(06:01 GMT) *MTU Aero Engines 1Q Rev EUR2.23B
(06:01 GMT) *Analysts Saw MTU Aero Engines 1Q Revenue at EUR2.22B
(06:02 GMT) *MTU Aero Engines 1Q EBIT EUR303M
(06:02 GMT) *MTU Aero Engines 1Q Adj EBIT EUR320M
(06:02 GMT) *Analysts Saw MTU Aero Engines 1Q Adj EBIT at EUR310M
(06:02 GMT) *MTU Aero Engines 1Q Adj EBIT Margin 14.2%
(06:03 GMT) *MTU Aero Engines 1Q EBITDA EUR409M
(06:03 GMT) *MTU Aero Engines 1Q Order Backlog EUR31.55B
(06:04 GMT) *MTU Aero Engines 1Q Free Cash Flow EUR177M
(06:04 GMT) *MTU Aero Engines Targets Adjusted Revenue of Between EUR9.2B - EUR9.7B
(06:06 GMT) *MTU Aero Engines Targets 2026 Adjusted EBIT EUR1.35B - EUR1.45B
(06:07 GMT) *MTU Aero Engines 1Q Net Pft EUR200M
(06:36 GMT) MTU Aero Engines Revenue Rises on Strength in Military, Commercial Maintenance Businesses

By Nina Kienle

MTU Aero Engines' revenue rose in the first three months of the year, boosted by strength in its military business and commercial maintenance business.

The German aircraft-engine manufacturer on Thursday reported 200 million euros ($233.5 million) in net profit for the first quarter, a 11% decrease compared with the prior-year period. On an adjusted basis, net profit was up 3% on year to 229 million euros.

Revenue, meanwhile, rose 6% to 2.23 billion euros.

Adjusted earnings before interest and taxes--a key profitability metric--increased 6% on year to 320 million euros.

"There was no impact on our first-quarter figures from the war in Iran," Chief Financial Officer Katja Garcia Vila said.

MTU Aero Engines confirmed its guidance for 2026, despite rising uncertainty in light of the conflict in the Middle East. The group is targeting adjusted revenue of between 9.2 billion and 9.7 billion euros. Adjusted EBIT is expected to be between 1.35 billion and 1.45 billion euros.

The group's order backlog stood at 31.55 billion euros at the end of March.

Write to Nina Kienle at nina.kienle@wsj.com

data.title: WTB: Adjusted EBITDAR up 4% as accommodation growth offsets F&B decline; Germany turns profitable
data.description:
Group revenue was flat year-over-year as UK and Germany accommodation sales offset lower F&B revenue, with adjusted EBITDAR up 4% and Premier Inn Germany achieving its first annual profit. A new Five-Year Plan aims to boost margins and returns, while inflation and AGP-related impairments weighed on statutory profit.

Original document: Whitbread PLC [WTB] Annual Financial Statement — Apr. 30 2026
